title: Filing a consumer complaint — District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
text: Under the Consumer Protection Act 2019, a consumer can file a complaint before the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission for goods or services where the value of the goods or services paid as consideration does not exceed one crore rupees. A complaint can be filed by the consumer to whom goods are sold or services are rendered, any recognised consumer association, the Central Government or State Government, or one or more consumers on behalf of numerous consumers having the same interest. Under Section 35, a complaint shall be filed in the District Commission within the local limits of whose jurisdiction the opposite party resides, carries on business, or the cause of action arose. A complaint can be filed electronically through the e-daakhil portal at edaakhil.nic.in. No advocate is required to file a consumer complaint — any consumer can file the complaint personally. The complaint must contain the name and address of the complainant and the opposite party, facts relating to the complaint and when the cause of action arose, documents in support of the allegations, and the relief claimed. The District Commission shall refer a copy of the complaint to the opposite party and direct them to give their version within 30 days. If the complaint is admitted, the Commission proceeds to settle the dispute. The Commission can order replacement or repair of defective goods, refund of the price paid, payment of compensation for loss or injury, discontinuation of unfair trade practices, and payment of punitive damages.
